title.en: PERKOnto: Personal Email Research Knowledge Ontology

description.en: A domain-specific ontology for representing the professional  knowledge of a researcher as extractable from personal email  communications. PERKOnto models communication infrastructure, researchers and venues, the publication lifecycle, and research content,  anchoring every assertion to its provenance email.

title.value: PERKOnto: Personal Email Research Knowledge Ontology

comment.en: An individual email message exchanged between researchers.  Serves as the provenance anchor for all knowledge triples extracted into  PERK. Every relationship carries a source property referencing one or  more Email instances.
A bibliographic metadata record for a published paper,  capturing identifiers, venue, and publication details.
A computational or analytical method, model, or algorithm  discussed in the email corpus.
A composite identifier linking a specific paper to a  specific submission instance at a target venue, enabling tracking of  multiple parallel or sequential submissions.
An ordered chain of email messages sharing a common subject  and thread identifier.

A node representing a single state in the submission  lifecycle of a paper (e.g. Submitted, Under Review, Revision Required,  Accepted, Rejected). Status nodes are chained via movesTo to encode  the full submission history.
A scheduled meeting between researchers, including virtual  meetings, with an associated agenda and link.
A research paper authored or co-authored by one or more  Persons in the email corpus.
An evaluation metric used to assess the performance of a  method on a task or dataset.
A dataset used or discussed in the research activities  documented in the email corpus.
An academic conference to which a paper has been submitted  or which a researcher attends.
An academic journal to which a paper has been submitted  or published.
A research task or problem formulation that datasets,  methods, or metrics are applied to.
A researcher or collaborator appearing as sender, recipient,  or named entity in the email corpus.
Links an Email to the Person(s) who received it.
Links a Paper to one of its author Persons.
Links a Method to a Dataset it is trained or evaluated  on.
The submission state label, e.g. 'Submitted',  'Under Review', 'Revision Required', 'Accepted', 'Rejected'.
The date on which the task was assigned or initiated.
Digital Object Identifier for a published paper.
Links a Person to a Meeting or Conference they attend.
The date on which the Email was sent.
A unique string identifier for a MailThread.
Links a SubmissionID to its initial PaperStatus, or one  PaperStatus to the next, encoding the submission lifecycle as a directed  state machine.
Links a Dataset or Method to the Task it is applied to.
The institutional affiliation of a Person.
Links a SubmissionID to the Paper it identifies.
Links a SubmissionID to its target Conference or Journal.
Links a Person to a Task they are working on.
Links an Email to the Person who sent it.
Links a Metric to the Task, Method, or Dataset it  evaluates.
The date on which this status was recorded.
Links a Person to a Method, Dataset, or Metric they use  in their research.
Version string of the manuscript (e.g. arXiv v2).
Links an Email to the MailThread it belongs to.
Links a Paper to its bibliographic record.
A unique integer identifier for an Email.
